metadata.scope: AI-assisted perception robustness qualification for Royal Navy USV programmes; AMLAS/SACE safety case

outline.rationale: Royal Navy USV perception programme flows through 7 sequential phases (Phase 1: Requirements Decomposition through Phase 7: In-Service Monitoring). Organizing Section 3 by phase aligns with programme lifecycle, supports Dstl audit traceability (monthly reports per phase gate), enables clear assignment of evidence artifacts to phase deliverables, and maintains bidirectional traceability (requirement → phase → scenarios → acceptance criteria → evidence artifacts). This hierarchical structure is more natural for T&E programmes than flat requirement lists and supports impact analysis (if Phase 3 timeline slips, which Phase 4/5/6 requirements are affected?).

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content_summary: Purpose, scope, definitions, standards, and overview of AI-assisted Test & Evaluation system for autonomous maritime perception stack.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content_summary: The system shall provide AI-assisted test decomposition, scenario automation, evidence generation, and safety-case drafting for the Royal Navy USV autonomous collision-avoidance perception stack, accelerating T&E timeline from 18 months (manual effort) to 12 months (AI-assisted) while maintaining Dstl compliance with AMLAS and JSP 520.

subsections.title: Scope
subsections.gap_notes: Add excluded systems (mission planning outside scope), geographic scope (UK test ranges: BUTEC, Portland, Pentland Firth), performance envelope (Beaufort sea states 2-5, visibility 50-500 m, daylight-only operation in v1.0).
subsections.content_summary: In scope: Requirements decomposition (Phase 1), Data curation & synthesis (Phase 2), Model evaluation & red-teaming (Phase 3), HIL validation (Phase 4), Physical trials (Phase 5), Safety-case generation (Phase 6), Post-deployment drift monitoring (Phase 7). Out of scope: Mission planning, navigation stack, USV hardware qualification, commercial sensor certification.

subsections.title: Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations
subsections.gap_notes: Verify 18 unverified terms (XLUUV, DAU, RAISO, NavyX) against UK Defence standards. Add version numbers for cited standards.
subsections.content_summary: 55 verified terms including AMLAS (Assurance & Monitoring of Learning-enabled Autonomous Systems), ODD (Operational Design Domain), PSRQ (Phased Safety & Requirements Qualification), SACE (Structured Assurance Case Environment), GSN (Goal Structured Notation), Dstl (Defence Science and Technology Laboratory), JSP 520 (UK Defence manual of standards for security), DEFSTAN 00-056 (UK Defence standard for AI assurance).

subsections.title: References
subsections.gap_notes: Formalize with version numbers and URIs: JSP 520 v3.2 (2023), DEFSTAN 00-056 v1.0, AMLAS York CfAA technical report, OpenSCENARIO 2.0 spec URI, tool version constraints (Isaac Sim ≥4.0, MLflow ≥2.0, Foolbox ≥4.0).
subsections.content_summary: UK Defence standards: JSP 520, DEFSTAN 00-056, RA 1600. AI assurance: AMLAS (York CfAA), UK Defence AI Playbook. Test frameworks: OpenSCENARIO 2.0, Scenic DSL. Tools: Isaac Sim, Gazebo, NVIDIA TAO, Foolbox, CleverHans, SHAP, LIME, Grad-CAM, Garak, PyRIT, MLflow. Standards: Article 36 (UK autonomous systems legal review), UK Collision Regulations (Maritime Rules).

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.gap_notes: Add ROI/cost-benefit analysis: manual T&E 18 months @ ~15 FTE = 2700 engineer-months; AI-assisted 12 months @ ~8 FTE = 960 engineer-months (64% reduction). Cost of AI tooling (~£2M) vs cost avoidance.
subsections.content_summary: AI-assisted T&E accelerates requirements decomposition (3-5× speedup vs manual), scenario authoring (10 scenarios/engineer-day vs 1 manual scenario/day), GSN safety-case drafting (weeks to days), and drift detection/re-qualification (continuous automated monitoring). Expected benefits: reduced schedule (18→12 months), reduced manual effort, improved traceability for Dstl audit, repeatable evidence generation on model changes.

sections.title: Overall Description
sections.content_summary: Product perspective (system interfaces, user roles, constraints, assumptions), product functions, and apportioning of requirements to subsystems.

subsections.title: Product Perspective - System Interfaces
subsections.gap_notes: Critical gap. Add (a) Isaac Sim API binding (Gym interface for sensor sim), (b) ROS 2 DDS interface schema (collision-avoidance stack integration), (c) AUVLAB hardware sensor-bench protocol, (d) classified-data fabric (DAIS/Crown Hosting API for evidence store), (e) naval network comms interface (Gigabit Ethernet, encrypted tunnel).
subsections.content_summary: TO BE POPULATED: Interface specifications for Isaac Sim/Gazebo (simulation platform bindings), ROS 2 (middleware for perception-to-autonomy stack), MLOps databases (MLflow, DVC), classified-data infrastructure (MOD DAIS, Crown Hosting), AUVLAB hardware sensor bench, external AI tools (NVIDIA TAO, Foolbox, Garak).

subsections.title: Product Perspective - User Interfaces
subsections.gap_notes: Add UI specifications for (a) operator override interface (button vs touchscreen vs voice?), (b) GSN authoring UI (form-based templating vs drag-drop canvas vs text editor?), (c) T&E Engineer dashboard (decomposition progress, data curation status, test execution), (d) Dstl safety engineer review portal (evidence approval workflows).
subsections.content_summary: T&E Engineer: requirements decomposition tool (markdown input → atomic clause output), scenario authoring (Scenic DSL editor or template library), data curation dashboard (ODD cell coverage heatmap). Dstl Safety Engineer: evidence review portal (test results, robustness bounds, explainability reports with approval checkboxes). NavyX Operator: drift-alert dashboard (SLO status, override control).

subsections.title: Product Perspective - Hardware Interfaces
subsections.gap_notes: Missing GPU specs (NVIDIA A100 80GB for training vs V100 32GB?), classified-infra SLA (MOD DAIS region, failover latency), sensor-bench hardware API (voltage/timing/signal format for AUVLAB ingestion), power/cooling constraints (e.g., onboard USV GPU constraints vs ground station), redundancy (backup compute).
subsections.content_summary: TO BE POPULATED: GPU specifications for model training (estimated 8× A100 @ 40GB VRAM each for parallel training), classified-infrastructure hosting (MOD DAIS or Crown Hosting certification), AUVLAB hardware interface (sensor-bench serial/Ethernet protocol), evidence-store storage (multi-PB archive over 7 years), USV-onboard compute specs for live drift detection.

subsections.title: Product Perspective - Software Interfaces
subsections.gap_notes: Add API version constraints (Isaac Sim 4.0+, MLflow 2.0+, Foolbox 4.0+, CleverHans 0.1+, SHAP 0.43+, Garak 0.10+, PyRIT 0.3+), serialization formats (protobuf vs JSON for evidence artifacts), error handling (timeout policies for long-running Phase 3 evaluations), licence compatibility audit.
subsections.content_summary: 18 tool integrations documented: Isaac Sim/Gazebo (scenario execution), NVIDIA TAO (domain adaptation), StyleGAN/diffusion (synthetic data augmentation), MLflow/DVC (lineage tracking), Foolbox/CleverHans (adversarial robustness), SHAP/LIME/Grad-CAM (explainability), Garak/PyRIT (red-teaming), D-Case Editor (GSN authoring), SACE (evidence templates), git (version control).

subsections.title: Product Perspective - Communications Interfaces
subsections.gap_notes: Missing network protocol (HTTP/S, gRPC, custom TCP for evidence sync?), encryption (TLS 1.3? IPsec VPN?), latency SLA (drift alert delivery <1 hour to Dstl = 30 sec upload + processing time budget), bandwidth (evidence batches ≤5MB per REQ-013, but upload interval 6 hours → 10 KB/s average).
subsections.content_summary: TO BE POPULATED: Classified-network comms protocol for evidence upload (encrypted tunnel to Dstl server), latency SLA (drift alerts <1 hour to Dstl team lead), bandwidth constraints (onboard USV upload 10 KB/s average), cross-domain marking (AUKUS interoperability if NATO allies access), network redundancy (failover to SMS alerts if primary comms down).

subsections.title: Product Perspective - Memory & Storage Constraints
subsections.gap_notes: Model size (object-detection checkpoint ~500 MB for v1.0?), evidence-store sizing (7-year archive of sea-trial videos, evaluation logs, etc. = multi-PB scale?), RAM for inference (onboard USV vs ground station), batch-processing memory (synthetic data generation batch size = function of GPU VRAM).
subsections.content_summary: TO BE POPULATED: Model checkpoint size (estimate 500 MB – 2 GB for production object detector), training batch size (128–256 images @ ~20 MB/image = 2.5–5 GB GPU VRAM per batch), evidence-archive size (7-year retention of Phase 5 trial video logs, Phase 3 test results ≈ 100 TB–1 PB), onboard USV compute (drift detection inference <500 ms on edge GPU, <50 MB model).

subsections.title: Product Perspective - Operations & Maintenance
subsections.gap_notes: Add backup/restore procedures (evidence store recovery RTO/RPO), disaster recovery (if classified server compromised, model suppression failsafe?), maintenance windows (model updates without disrupting sea trials), on-call support model (24/7 Dstl availability? 24/5 weekday-only?).
subsections.content_summary: Normal operation: Phase 1-6 CI/CD pipeline (nightly Phase 3 evaluation, Phase 4 HIL validation, auto-update GSN). Phase 7 operational deployment: continuous drift monitoring every 5 minutes, alert escalation SOP, RAISO override request routing. Maintenance: model retraining triggers re-qualification (5-working-day cycle per REQ-014). Incident response: if ODD exceedance detected, auto-suppress model, escalate to RAISO.

subsections.title: Product Perspective - Site Adaptation
subsections.gap_notes: Site-specific ODD calibration (Pentland Firth tidal flow, Beaufort scale correlation to local sea-state data), local traffic models (UK shipping lane patterns per Lloyd's Register), RF interference maps (MOD-specific frequency coordination), maritime rules (UK Collision Regulations vs NATO STANAG). Affects Phase 5 trial planning.
subsections.content_summary: TO BE POPULATED: Adaptation rules for UK test ranges (BUTEC, Portland, Pentland Firth): local ODD calibration (weather patterns, typical vessel traffic, RF environment), sensor drift baseline (salinity, temperature correlation), operator training & handover procedures per range. Post-deployment adaptation: if USV transferred to NATO ally waters, re-calibrate ODD envelope and re-run Phase 5 validation.

subsections.title: Product Functions
subsections.content_summary: 16 primary functions mapped to 7 phases: Phase 1 (REQ-001), Phase 2 (REQ-002, REQ-003), Phase 3 (REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-009), Phase 4 (REQ-008), Phase 5 (REQ-010), Phase 6 (REQ-011, REQ-012), Phase 7 (REQ-013). Cross-phase functions: REQ-014 (MLOps lineage), REQ-015 (RBAC/classified data), REQ-016 (CI/CD integration).

subsections.title: User Characteristics
subsections.gap_notes: Add skill/experience profiles: T&E Engineer (PhD in ML OR 10+ years test automation?), Dstl Safety Engineer (certified AMLAS auditor?), NavyX Operator (maritime training + override-interface training duration?), accessibility (vision/hearing support for override button?).
subsections.content_summary: Primary users: T&E Engineer (12+ years test automation + AI/ML literacy, workflow: decompose → curate → author scenarios → analyze results), Dstl Safety Engineer (UK Defence certification, AMLAS competency, workflow: review evidence → approve gates → sign GSN), RAISO (Royal Assurance & Insurance Service Officer, override authority, ~1 per programme). Secondary: NavyX operators (sea-trial execution, override response <2 seconds assumed).

subsections.title: Constraints
subsections.gap_notes: Formalize constraint hierarchy: hard (non-negotiable) vs soft (desirable). Add cost budget (estimated £15M–25M programme cost?), schedule contingency (PSRQ Phase 1-6 in 12 months; Phase 7 in-service post-deployment = +24 months), data-residency rules (UK-only or NATO-shareable?), regulatory constraints (GDPR for sea-trial operator PII, ITAR for NVIDIA export controls).
subsections.content_summary: Hard constraints: ODD coverage ≥95% (≥228/240 cells, REQ-003), detection latency ≤500 ms (REQ-008), classification accuracy ≥85% mAP (REQ-004), adversarial robustness ≥20 pp drop tolerance (REQ-005), operator override response <2 s (REQ-008), Article 36 legal approval mandatory (REQ-012). Soft constraints: 3-5× decomposition speedup (REQ-001), 10 scenarios/engineer-day (REQ-009), GSN generation <2 working days (REQ-011). PSRQ timeline: Phase 1-6 in 12 months, Phase 7 operational (ongoing).

subsections.title: Assumptions and Dependencies
subsections.gap_notes: Formalize dependencies: SLA for Dstl safety-engineer availability (assumed <5 days per review, needs explicit SLA), GPU compute availability (assumed 'classified GPU compute' exists and accessible; need MOU with UK Gov), regulatory approval (Article 36 legal review assumed willing and timely; need MOU), third-party tools (Isaac Sim license perpetual vs annual subscription? Risk?). Add fallback plans.
subsections.content_summary: Assumed available: Dstl safety engineer review capacity (≤5 working days per gate review, 6 gates = 30 engineer-days total). MOD-certified GPU compute (≥8× A100 equivalent, accessible within 2-week procurement lead-time). UK Defence legal office availability for Article 36 review (≤3 working days for approval decision). Third-party tools perpetually licensed or internally hosted (NVIDIA Isaac Sim, Gazebo, MLflow, Foolbox). Sea-trial range access (BUTEC, Portland, Pentland Firth availability per schedule). Operator training capacity (24 operators × 8 hours override-interface training = 192 hours, assumed completed before Phase 5).

subsections.title: Apportioning of Requirements
subsections.gap_notes: Missing subsystem allocation. Define who owns 85% mAP target: data-curation layer (REQ-002, REQ-003) vs model-training layer vs inference pipeline? Latency budget allocation (500 ms total: inference 200 ms? Serialization 50 ms? Comms 250 ms?). False-positive vs false-negative trade-off allocation. Evidence-assurance levels (which evidence requires independent audit vs self-test?). Define subsystems: Data Pipeline (REQ-001–003), Model Layer (REQ-004–007), System Integration (REQ-008–010), Documentation (REQ-011–012), Operations (REQ-013–014), Security (REQ-015–016).
subsections.content_summary: TO BE POPULATED: Subsystem decomposition and requirement allocation. Example: Data Curation subsystem (REQ-002, REQ-003) owns ≥228/240 ODD cells populated; Model Evaluation subsystem (REQ-004, REQ-005) owns ≥85% mAP and adversarial robustness bounds; System Integration subsystem (REQ-008, REQ-010) owns end-to-end latency ≤1 s (inference 200 ms + decision logic 300 ms + comms 500 ms). Define inter-subsystem interfaces and dependencies.

sections.title: Specific Requirements
sections.content_summary: 16 functional requirements organized by 7 PSRQ phases, with 28 acceptance scenarios, sub-features, and traceability to evidence artifacts. Cross-phase requirements (MLOps, RBAC, CI/CD) integrated as horizontal concerns.

subsections.title: Phase 1 — Requirements Decomposition
subsections.content_summary: Automated decomposition of ODD, safety, and mission requirements into atomic testable conditions. Success criterion: 50+ top-level requirements decomposed in <15 minutes with ≥95% traceability coverage. Output: atomic clauses, traceability matrix, SACE XML. Gate: 100% acceptance by Dstl T&E lead. Exception handling: underspecified ODD flags human clarification workflow (SCEN-003).
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: ≥95% atomic clauses linked to named requirement and AMLAS technique ID; zero untraced clauses in traceability matrix; human clarification for any ambiguous requirements completed within 5 working days.

subsections.title: Phase 2 — Data Curation & Synthetic Augmentation
subsections.content_summary: Ingest 500+ GB real sensor logs (AUVLAB, Dstl sea trials) and generate synthetic data for ODD coverage closure. Real data: ≥228/240 ODD cells populated (95% coverage) by end of Phase 2. Synthetic data: ≥10,000 frames per condition, full provenance logged (tool version, seed, hyperparameters, QA sign-off). Exception: provenance gaps block SACE linking; reject batch and require re-work (SCEN-005). Edge case: diffusion inpainting for sensor occlusion (SCEN-006, human validation ≥4/5 physics-plausibility).
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: ≥228/240 ODD cells populated with ≥1 real log each; all synthetic batches linked to MLflow run_id with full provenance within 24 hours of generation; human QA sign-off on 100% of synthetic batches (physics-plausible); zero provenance-gap rejections at SACE ingestion.

subsections.title: Phase 3 — Verification (Model Evaluation, Robustness, Red-teaming, Explainability)
subsections.content_summary: Comprehensive model verification: (1) Accuracy: ≥85% mAP@0.5 per class, ECE ≤0.05 on held-out test set (SCEN-021); (2) Robustness: adversarial bounds filed per class, accuracy drop ≤20 pp at ε=0.1, ≤40 pp at ε=0.2 (SCEN-007); pairwise robustness under combined ODD conditions (SCEN-009); (3) Red-teaming: ≥50 adversarial scenarios (spoofing, jamming, morphing); >30 pp accuracy drop triggers RCA (SCEN-027); LLM jailbreak detection <500 ms (SCEN-028); (4) Explainability: ≥95% test failures explained via SHAP+Grad-CAM; spurious features <5% (SCEN-024); Grad-CAM saliency alignment with safety-case narrative checked (SCEN-025); (5) Scenario automation: ≥240 Scenic scenarios generated & compiled in <30 min per 100-scenario batch (SCEN-018–020).
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: Accuracy: mAP ≥85% all classes, ECE ≤0.05, no ODD cell >10 pp accuracy drop below macro-average. Robustness: all adversarial bounds met (≤20/40 pp drops), 95% CI on degradation computed, Dstl security engineer sign-off. Red-teaming: 50+ scenarios executed, RCA filed for >30 pp drop cases within 3 working days, RAISO override issued if RCA acceptance risk. Explainability: ≥95% failures explained, <5% spurious features, Grad-CAM alignment ≥90%. Scenario automation: ≥240 scenarios, execution latency ≤4 hours for full suite or graceful partial-results timeout. Gate decision: ALL criteria must pass for Phase 4 approval; otherwise escalate to RAISO exception process (REQ-015.1).

subsections.title: Phase 4 — Validation (Hardware-in-the-Loop, Digital Twin)
subsections.content_summary: Closed-loop HIL simulation of collision-avoidance system. Real perception stack integrated with simulated USV dynamics (Isaac Sim/Gazebo), synthetic sensor streams. Test scenarios: vessel approach, emergency stop, traffic crossing (≥50 scenarios). Success criteria: (1) ≥98% scenarios avoid collision (minimum separation >0 m after evasion); (2) Detection latency ≤500 ms, decision latency ≤1 s from threat detection to evasion command (100% of scenarios); (3) Degraded-sensor fallback (GPS loss, LiDAR dropout) triggers within 500 ms; (4) Human handover response <2 s (operator assumes control within 2 s of request, 95% of scenarios, SCEN-011); (5) Sensor dropout re-acquisition <3 s, false-positive rate <5% in high-complexity scenarios (SCEN-012). All simulation logs, telemetry, video, Dstl safety engineer review before Phase 5 gate.
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: ≥50 collision-avoidance scenarios executed in HIL; ≥95% avoid collision (relaxed from 98% as 'confidence metric'); latency ≤1 s from threat to evasion command (100%); degraded-sensor fallback confirmed within 500 ms; operator handover <2 s (95%); re-acquisition <3 s, FP <5%. Simulation evidence (video, telemetry logs) stored in SACE evidence template. Dstl safety engineer approval mandatory. RAISO exception authority if <95% pass rate (target 98%, but gating at 95%). Go/no-go gate: if all latency + fallback criteria met, proceed to Phase 5; otherwise escalate to model hardening or ODD restriction analysis.

subsections.title: Phase 5 — Real-World Validation (Targeted Physical Trials)
subsections.content_summary: Targeted sea trials at BUTEC or Pentland Firth to anchor synthetic evidence. Trial plan specifies: (1) Weather anchors (Beaufort sea states 3–4, rain, fog): ≥3 conditions, minimum 2 hours on water per condition; (2) Littoral traffic anchors (UK shipping corridors): ≥5 real vessel encounters per condition (fishing, merchant, nav-hazard), benchmarked against Phase 4 predictions; (3) Sensor drift anchors: ≥3 environment variables (sea state, visibility, illumination) measured over 30-day at-sea mission. Success criterion: sim-to-real accuracy gap ≤10 pp per ODD cell tested in both phases (SCEN-022); if >10 pp, RCA filed within 5 working days (causes: ODD exceedance, sensor drift, overfitting). All physical-trial data (video, telemetry, comparison tables) stored in classified archive per JSP 520. Dstl approval gate before Phase 6.
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: ≥3 weather-state conditions tested; ≥5 real vessel encounters per condition recorded; sensor drift measured on ≥3 variables over 30-day mission. Sim-to-real comparison: accuracy gap ≤10 pp for all ODD cells tested in both Phase 4 and Phase 5. If >10 pp gap: RCA initiated within 5 working days, resolution (retraining / ODD restriction / trial repetition) determined within 10 working days. Gate decision: if all conditions tested + gap ≤10 pp (or RCA accepted), proceed to Phase 6; if persistent >10 pp gap unresolved after 2 RCA cycles, escalate to RAISO exception (requires justification for operational release without perfect alignment).

subsections.title: Phase 6 — Safety Case & Documentation (Evidence Bundle, GSN, Legal Review)
subsections.content_summary: Auto-generate AMLAS-structured evidence bundle and GSN safety case from Phase 3–5 artifacts. (1) AAES (Activity Evidence Summary) table: map 6 AMLAS activities to evidence items (requirements → test data → model → evaluation results → bounds → safety argument). (2) GSN draft in D-Case Editor XML: claims (e.g., 'Model robust under ODD envelope'), evidence (test results, adversarial bounds), strategies (defence-in-depth: model + ODD restriction + fallback). Success criterion: GSN draft within 48 hours post-Phase 5; ≥95% top-level claims linked to ≥1 evidence item (SCEN-016); zero broken traceability links; 6/6 AMLAS activities represented. Exception handling (SCEN-017): human rejects claim due to inference gap → system re-generates within 2 working days with missing link. After 2 rejection cycles, escalate to RAISO for acceptance. (3) Article 36 legal export (REQ-012): PDF executive summary + SACE XML machine-readable format submitted to UK Defence legal office. Legal review approval gate mandatory before Phase 7 operational release.
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: GSN auto-generated within 48 hours of Phase 5 completion; ≥95% claims linked to evidence (zero dangling claims); bidirectional traceability (requirement ↔ test ↔ evidence ↔ claim) intact; 6/6 AMLAS activities in AAES table; Dstl safety engineer approval on draft (first or second iteration). Article 36 export: legal-office submission within 3 working days of GSN approval; legal approval gate must be passed before PSRQ sign-off. If rejected: re-submit within 10 working days with modifications addressing legal concerns.

subsections.title: Phase 7 — Post-Deployment Monitoring (Live Drift Detection, Continuous Re-qualification)
subsections.content_summary: Onboard USV drift detection on 3 ODD variables: (1) Sea State (Beaufort scale inferred from radar spectral moments); (2) Visibility (optical fog density, contrast-normalized LiDAR range); (3) Sea-clutter magnitude (RCS dB, radar reference cell). Monitoring: every 5 minutes compute drift scores, upload batches (≤5 MB) to Dstl classified evidence server every 6 hours. Alert thresholds: ±2σ band from baseline (30-day pre-deployment calibration). Escalation logic (SCEN-013–015): (a) SLO-yellow (1–2 variables exceed ±2σ for 3 consecutive uploads = 15 min window): increase collision-avoidance safety margins by 250 m (SCEN-014), notify Dstl team lead within 1 hour, email SLA <1 hour. (b) SLO-red (3 variables exceed ±2σ OR single variable >3σ): auto-restrict ODD envelope conservatively (reduce declared operating range), notify RAISO. (c) Auto-suppress (unresolved SLO-red for 12 hours without RAISO override): disable autonomous collision-avoidance, revert to manual-only operation (SCEN-015). RAISO override process: submit justification + approval signature, log in evidence store, auditable with 100% traceability. All drift logs retained (≥30 days live, ≥1 year archive per JSP 520).
subsections.phase_gate_criteria: Drift monitor deployed onboard before sea trials. Pre-deployment baseline established (30-day data). Alert timing: ±2σ detection within 15 minutes of threshold crossing; SLO-yellow notification <1 hour; SLO-red escalation to RAISO <30 minutes. False-positive rate <5% on 30-day baseline (to avoid nuisance alerts). Auto-suppress logic: 12-hour countdown confirmed operational; RAISO override request triggers within 1 minute of override action. Evidence store sync: drift logs uploaded every 6 hours with <5-minute processing latency. Monthly audit by Dstl security officer: check 100% of drift alerts, override actions, re-qualification decisions for compliance.

subsections.title: Cross-Phase Requirements: MLOps Lineage, RBAC & Data Security, CI/CD Integration
subsections.content_summary: REQ-014 (MLOps Lineage): Track model version (git commit, training date, hyperparameters) linked to all evidence artifacts. Evidence artifact schema: ID, model version, dataset version, approval status (pending/approved/rejected/superseded). Lineage stored in MLOps database (MLflow/DVC) + synced to SACE. On model retraining: auto-mark old evidence 'superseded', trigger Phase 3 re-evaluation, auto-update AAES & GSN. Sync latency ≤5 minutes. Model retrain-to-re-evidence cycle <5 working days. All versions retained indefinitely in archive. REQ-015 (RBAC & Classified Data): Access roles: T&E Engineer, Dstl Safety Engineer, RAISO, NavyX Operator (read-only drift alerts, can override auto-suppress). All access logged (actor, action, timestamp, data item). Classified data stored on MOD-certified infrastructure (DAIS or Crown Hosting) with encryption at-rest + in-transit. Data-sharing agreements required for external tool exports (Isaac Sim, NVIDIA TAO). Monthly security audit by Dstl officer; access violations trigger revocation. REQ-016 (CI/CD Integration): AMLAS artifact pipeline (Kubeflow/Jenkins/GitHub Actions) auto-triggers on every model commit: Phase 1 decomposition (if requirements change), Phase 3 evaluation (<4 h completion), Phase 4 HIL validation (<24 h completion), auto-generate GSN updates. Full audit trail: requirement → code → test → evidence → approval. Phase 3 gate failure blocks deployment (RAISO override required). Monthly traceability audits by Dstl verify zero missed artifacts.
subsections.cross_phase_dependencies: REQ-014 enables REQ-016 CI/CD automation (lineage data feeds GSN re-generation). REQ-015 gates REQ-014 & REQ-016 (evidence store encryption, role-based access to lineage data). All three are foundational for Phase 3–7 continuous assurance & re-qualification cycles.

subsections.title: Appendix C — PSRQ Phase Gates & Gate Criteria
subsections.content_summary: Summary of success criteria for each phase (Phase 1-7). Gate owner (Dstl Safety Engineer or RAISO), approval timeline, escalation path if gate fails. Linked to SCEN acceptance criteria.

subsections.content_summary: Inventory of evidence artifacts generated by each requirement: test result logs, simulation videos, robustness bounds tables, GSN drafts, legal export PDFs, drift-alert logs. Storage location (classified archive), retention period (JSP 520 compliance), access control (RBAC per REQ-015).

subsections.title: Appendix E — Exception Handling & RAISO Override Process
subsections.content_summary: Formal escalation path for requirements failures: (1) gate failure detection, (2) RCA investigation, (3) RAISO override request (with justification), (4) approval signature + audit trail logging. Timeline: gate failure → RCA <5 working days → override decision <10 working days. See amelia-findings.json finding #10 for full spec.

subsections.title: Appendix F — Definitions, Acronyms, Abbreviations (Full Glossary)
subsections.content_summary: 55 verified terms (AMLAS, ODD, PSRQ, SACE, GSN, etc.) + 18 unverified (XLUUV, DAU, RAISO, NavyX). To be verified against UK Defence standards & linked to IEEE 830 § 1.3.

outline.summary: IEEE 830 SRS for Royal Navy USV autonomous collision-avoidance perception T&E programme, organized by 7 functional hierarchy (PSRQ phases). 16 requirements, 28 scenarios, identified 10 critical gaps in Section 2 (interfaces, hardware, constraints, apportioning) and 2 major redundancies (synthetic data provenance, robustness testing scope). Full elaboration of Section 2 required before implementation freeze.

modifiability_concerns.mitigation: Populate 2.1–2.6 with detailed subsections per IEEE 830 template. Allocate requirements to subsystems. Define interface control documents for Isaac Sim, ROS 2, classified-data fabric.
Clarify boundary: REQ-005 is pixel-space adversarial perturbations; REQ-006 is physical-world scenario stressing. Define conflict resolution precedence if bounds disagree.
Merge into unified requirement; mandate MLflow integration; define schema for immutable provenance tagging (image EXIF + MLOps database).
Formalize gate precedence: Phase 4 gating at 95% pass rate (target 98%); Phase 5 precedence: real-world evidence overrides simulation. Document escalation to RAISO if conflicts.
Add REQ-015.1 defining exception categories, approval criteria, override duration limits, audit-trail format. Link to SCEN-015 and SCEN-017.
