U.finding_title: Vague temporal qualifier in deployment timeline
U.original_text: GSN safety case drafting reduced from weeks to days
U.ambiguity_explanation: How many days? 1–3 days? 3–7 days? No quantified threshold. 'Weeks to days' is relative and depends on case complexity, not absolute.
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall auto-generate an AMLAS-structured GSN safety case draft that a qualified human reviewer can approve within 5 working days post-model qualification.
U.reasoning: Safety-critical domain requires bounded timelines tied to review gates. 'Days' is meaningless without minimum/maximum bounds and workday definition.
U.recommendation: Define threshold: propose 5 working days to GSN draft approval is realistic for human review. Make it testable.

U.finding_title: Undefined acceleration multiplier in requirements analysis
U.ambiguity_explanation: '3-5×' speedup relative to what baseline? Manual decomposition of what scope? Assumes baseline is known, but unmeasured. Is this wall-clock time or engineer-days?
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall reduce human effort to decompose ODD, safety, and mission requirements into Phase 1 testable conditions from an estimated 10 engineer-days (manual baseline) to 2–3 engineer-days via automated decomposition and traceability matrix generation.
U.reasoning: Multipliers are relative and depend on unmeasured baselines. PSRQ Phase 1 scope must be defined to make '3-5×' testable. Propose concrete before/after hours.
U.recommendation: Establish baseline manual effort for Requirements decomposition (Phase 1) in hours, then measure achieved reduction. 10→3 engineer-days is reasonable for a medium-scale USV perception stack.

U.finding_title: Undefined list for 'environment variables' in drift monitoring
U.original_text: Drift monitors live on ≥ 3 environment variables post-deployment
U.ambiguity_explanation: Which 3 variables? 'Environment variables' is plural and undefined. Could be: sea state, visibility, water temperature, sensor temperature, lighting, sea-clutter magnitude, salinity. No list, units, or thresholds. AC says '≥ 3', but which 3 of a potentially infinite set?
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall deploy live drift detection on these 3 environment variables: (1) Sea State (Beaufort scale 0–6, measured via radar backscatter), (2) Visibility (optical fog density, measured as contrast-normalized LiDAR range), (3) Sea-clutter magnitude (normalized RCS in dB, measured from co-located radar reference). Alert threshold: signal any variable exceeding historical ±2σ band for ≥5 consecutive frames.
U.reasoning: Drift detection is a safety-critical post-deployment function. AMLAS requires measurable, repeatable monitoring. Undefined variables make it untestable and unverifiable.
U.recommendation: Select 3 ODD dimensions most strongly correlated to perception error (sea state, visibility, clutter) and define measurement methods per variable. 5-frame window prevents transient noise from triggering false alarms.

U.finding_title: Passive voice obscures actor and trigger for robustness bounds filing
U.original_text: Adversarial robustness bounds filed for every class
U.ambiguity_explanation: Who files? When? What format? 'Filed' is passive and unclear. Does it mean: stored in MLOps repo? Uploaded to AMLAS evidence bundle? Manually reviewed by security engineer? No actor, no artifact, no completion condition.
U.proposed_rewrite: The T&E Engineer shall file adversarial robustness bounds for every object class (vessel, buoy, swimmer, debris, small-object, small-contact) in the MLOps evidence store by Phase 3 completion. Bounds shall be formatted per SACE template (min-max accuracy under perturbation, tested via Foolbox, confidence interval ≥95%), and human-approved by Dstl security engineer before PSRQ Phase 4 gate.
U.reasoning: Passive voice in safety-critical contexts is a compliance risk. Actor, trigger, artifact format, and approval gate must be explicit to enable traceability and audit.
U.recommendation: Define: actor (T&E Engineer), trigger (Phase 3 completion), format (SACE template), approval (Dstl security engineer), gate (before Phase 4). Use Foolbox as the measurement standard.

U.finding_title: Vague acceptance criterion '95% coverage' — coverage of which ODD dimensions?
U.original_text: Coverage of declared ODD states ≥ 95 % (physics-accurate sim + synthetic data)
U.ambiguity_explanation: 'Declared ODD states' is plural and undefined. Which states? All combinations of (weather × visibility × sea-clutter × vessel-type × speed)? 95% of how many states? A 10-dimensional ODD space is combinatorially large; '95%' may be impossible or meaningless.
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall cover ≥95% of the declared ODD envelope via combined physics-accurate simulation and synthetic data. ODD envelope is defined as all combinations of: (1) Sea State (Beaufort 0–4), (2) Visibility (contrast ratio 0.1–1.0), (3) Vessel Classes (fishing, merchant, nav-hazard, small-craft), (4) USV Speed (0–3 knots). Coverage measured by grid-cell occupancy: each (sea-state, visibility, vessel, speed) cell populated with ≥1 test scenario. Target: ≥228 of 240 cells covered (95%).
U.reasoning: Coverage metrics require well-defined denominators. Without specifying which ODD dimensions and how many discrete states per dimension, '95%' is unmeasurable. AMLAS requires evidence of ODD boundary closure.
U.recommendation: Define ODD envelope explicitly as low-dimensional grid (4–5 key dimensions). Use grid-cell occupancy as coverage metric. 228/240 cells is realistic for Phase 2–3 effort.

U.finding_title: Synonym collision: 'evaluation' vs. 'validation' vs. 'verification' — AMLAS-specific definitions required
U.original_text: Model-layer accuracy and calibration evaluation ... System-layer evaluation ... Real-world validation
U.ambiguity_explanation: AMLAS defines: Verification = conformance to spec (does model meet accuracy target?), Validation = fitness for use in ODD (does model work in real conditions?), Evaluation = broader assessment. Requirement uses 'evaluation' for model-layer and 'validation' for physical trials, but AMLAS means something specific. Collision risk: unclear what evidence is generated.
U.proposed_rewrite: Phase 3 (Model-layer) shall conduct AMLAS Verification: measure object-detection accuracy, calibration error, and adversarial robustness on held-out test sets. Phase 4 (System-layer) shall conduct AMLAS Validation: measure closed-loop collision-avoidance performance in digital-twin ODD scenarios. Phase 5 (Physical trials) shall conduct AMLAS Fitness-for-use assessment: measure perception-stack performance under UK weather and littoral traffic conditions not covered by Phase 4. Each phase shall produce traced evidence per AMLAS Activity checklist.
U.reasoning: AMLAS is prescriptive: Verification, Validation, and Fitness-for-use are distinct assurance activities with different evidence types. Using 'evaluation' generically obscures which AMLAS activity produces which evidence artifact.
U.recommendation: Replace generic 'evaluation' with AMLAS terminology: Verification (Phase 3), Validation (Phase 4), Fitness-for-use (Phase 5). Map each phase to AMLAS Activity checklist. This ensures compliance-ready evidence traceability.

U.original_text: Closed-loop simulation replaces most physical trials
U.ambiguity_explanation: 'Most' is undefined: 80%? 90%? If critical safety margins must be validated on water, simulation alone may not be compliant. AMLAS/SACE do not permit skipping real-world validation for all hazard classes.
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall generate synthetic closed-loop scenarios covering ODD envelope such that physical Phase 5 trials are limited to: (a) weather state validation (rain, fog, sea state >3), (b) UK-specific littoral traffic regimes, and (c) sensor drift post-deployment anchoring. All other ODD combinations shall be validated via digital twin (Phase 4) simulation.
U.reasoning: Safety assurance frameworks (AMLAS, SACE) require risk-driven allocation of simulation vs. real-world. 'Most' is not a risk criterion. Must define which ODD dimensions require physical anchor tests.
U.recommendation: Replace 'most' with explicit list of scenarios requiring Phase 5 water trials. Recommend: weather, littoral traffic, sensor drift. All other ODD combinations can remain digital-twin validated.

U.finding_title: Passive voice in drift detection escalation — no defined escalation actor or SLA
U.original_text: Drift detection alarm raised and escalated to re-qualification team
U.ambiguity_explanation: 'Raised' and 'escalated' by what system? To which team? In what time? What defines 're-qualification'? Are alarms logged, messaged, or both? No actor, no latency SLA, no escalation path.
U.proposed_rewrite: When the drift monitor detects any environment variable exceeding ±2σ band (Phase 7), the MLOps system shall: (1) log alert to evidence store, (2) notify Dstl re-qualification team lead via email within 1 hour, (3) auto-suppress model in-service use after 12 hours if human override not provided. Re-qualification shall assess whether ODD restrictions or model retraining are required before deployment resumption.
U.reasoning: Escalation latency is a safety property. Without an SLA and human actor, alarms can be missed or delayed. MLOps automation must enforce deterministic alert routing.
U.recommendation: Define: 1-hour notification SLA, 12-hour auto-suppress if not overridden, re-qualification criteria (ODD restriction vs. model retrain). Make escalation actor explicit (Dstl team lead).

U.finding_title: Vague 'continuous' in post-deployment monitoring — sampling frequency and retention policy undefined
U.original_text: In-service drift monitoring replaces periodic re-qualification
U.ambiguity_explanation: 'Continuous' monitoring sounds real-time, but onboard USV compute is constrained. How often? Every frame? Every minute? Every hour? Data retention: days, weeks, months? What happens to historical logs? No sampling frequency, no storage SLA, no archival policy.
U.proposed_rewrite: Post-deployment (Phase 7), the USV shall compute drift scores on the 3 monitored environment variables every 5 minutes (onboard) and upload batches (≤5MB) to the Dstl evidence server at 6-hour intervals over satellite/cellular link. Evidence server shall retain ≥30 days of drift telemetry. If any variable exceeds ±2σ band for ≥3 consecutive uploads (15 minutes), the system shall: (1) alert Dstl ops team, (2) downgrade model confidence in real-time, (3) auto-restrict ODD envelope until human override. Drift logs shall be retained in classified archive for ≥1 year per JSP 520.
U.reasoning: 'Continuous' in maritime autonomy is resource-constrained. Sampling frequency, communication latency, storage capacity, and archival must be specified. AMLAS and JSP 520 require audit trails.
U.recommendation: Define: sampling (5-min onboard), upload (6-hour batches), retention (30-day live + 1-year archive), alert threshold (3 consecutive excursions), escalation (Dstl ops + confidence downgrade). Align with JSP 520 data governance.

U.finding_title: Conjunction ambiguity: are all AI tools required or candidate list?
U.original_text: NVIDIA TAO / Isaac Sim / StyleGAN / diffusion / Unreal + VBS4 maritime for synthetic sensor data
U.ambiguity_explanation: Slash-list suggests alternatives, but budget/scope unclear. Must we integrate all? If TAO works, can we omit diffusion? NVIDIA tools vs. open-source (StyleGAN)? Dependency chains unknown: can Isaac Sim replace Unreal? Does Unreal + VBS4 require NVIDIA backend?
U.proposed_rewrite: For Phase 3 synthetic sensor data generation, the system shall integrate tool chain: (1) NVIDIA TAO for domain-adaptive object detection model training (required), (2) Isaac Sim OR Unreal + VBS4 for physics-accurate maritime environment rendering (select one based on sensor-fidelity requirements), (3) StyleGAN OR diffusion-based augmentation for texture variation (select one based on realism-vs-diversity trade-off). Tool selection rationale documented in MLOps configuration repo.
U.reasoning: Slash-lists in requirements create false optionality. Safety-critical data generation requires explicit tool selection and dependency management. Tool choice affects reproducibility and evidence lineage.
U.recommendation: Designate required vs. optional tools. For TAO: required (domain adaptation is non-negotiable). For rendering: select one (Isaac Sim for speed, Unreal+VBS4 for realism). For augmentation: select one based on ODD coverage gaps. Document rationale.

U.finding_title: Negation trap in GSN safety case approval — undefined fallback if rejected
U.original_text: GSN safety case draft auto-generated and human-approved
U.ambiguity_explanation: 'Auto-generated' + 'human-approved' implies approval succeeds. But what if rejected? Is there a remediation loop? How many rejection cycles allowed? What is failure mode? AMLAS requires iterative assurance refinement, but acceptance criterion says nothing about failure recovery.
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall auto-generate an AMLAS-structured GSN safety case draft by Phase 6. The draft shall be submitted for human approval by Dstl safety engineer. If approved: proceed to Phase 6 sign-off. If rejected: system shall log issues, engineer shall remediate, and system shall re-generate updated draft within 2 working days. After 2 rejection cycles: escalate to RAISO for exception decision. All GSN drafts (approved and rejected) retained in evidence store.
U.reasoning: Negation ('not approved') is implicit but critical. Safety-case approval is iterative in real projects. Requirement must define fallback loop, retry limit, and escalation to satisfy AMLAS traceability.
U.recommendation: Add rejection handling: remediation window (2 days), retry limit (2 cycles), escalation actor (RAISO). Ensure all drafts (pass/fail) are retained in evidence store for compliance audit.

U.finding_title: Vague qualifier '10× more scenarios' — scenarios per what unit?
U.original_text: Test case design 10× more scenarios per engineer-day
U.ambiguity_explanation: '10×' relative to what baseline? Manual scenario design via OpenSCENARIO? How many engineer-days? Is this 10 scenarios/day or 10× baseline (baseline unknown)? Does OpenSCENARIO 2.0 + Scenic reduce specification time or execution time?
U.proposed_rewrite: The system shall enable T&E Engineers to author ODD-compliant test scenarios via Scenic DSL such that scenario design throughput increases from baseline ~1 scenario/engineer-day (manual OpenSCENARIO) to ≥10 scenarios/engineer-day (AI-assisted template generation + Scenic compilation).
U.reasoning: Multipliers require baseline. Manual scenario authoring via OpenSCENARIO XML is laborious (~1/day). AI-assisted Scenic codegen can plausibly reach 10/day. Testable if we track engineer-hours and scenario count in MLOps telemetry.
U.recommendation: Establish baseline: 1 scenario/engineer-day via manual OpenSCENARIO authoring. Target: 10 scenarios/engineer-day via Scenic + AI templates. Track in MLOps metrics dashboard.

U.ambiguity_explanation: 'Standard' is subjective. Standard within Navy? Industry standard (IEEE, NIST)? Phase 3 of PSRQ? No metric, no gate, no compliance framework specified. Does 'standard' mean it's run every release or only once?
U.proposed_rewrite: Adversarial robustness testing shall be a standard Phase 3 activity in the PSRQ campaign. Every model candidate shall be assessed via Foolbox/CleverHans perturbations (FGSM, PGD, C&W attacks) on object classes (vessel, buoy, small-object) to derive accuracy bounds under 10%-pixel and 20%-pixel perturbation budgets. Bounds shall be filed in evidence store and reviewed by Dstl security engineer before Phase 4 gate. This activity shall be run on every model release.
U.reasoning: 'Becomes standard' is aspirational but unverifiable. Requirement must specify: activity (Phase 3), tools (Foolbox), metrics (perturbation budget), actors (Dstl reviewer), gate (Phase 4), and frequency (every release).
U.recommendation: Define adversarial testing as mandatory Phase 3 gate with: attack types (FGSM, PGD, C&W), perturbation budgets (10%, 20%), classes (vessel, buoy, small-object), approval actor (Dstl security), frequency (every model release).

U.finding_title: Passive voice and unclear trigger: 'will be filed' for adversarial bounds — no timestamp or actor
U.original_text: Adversarial robustness bounds will be filed for every class
U.ambiguity_explanation: Passive future tense: who files, when, where? 'Will be' suggests automation, but humans review. Is filing automatic or manual? Post-Phase 3? Before Phase 4 gate? No artifact location, no approval chain, no gate criterion.
U.proposed_rewrite: Upon Phase 3 completion, the MLOps system shall automatically write adversarial robustness bounds for every object class to the SACE evidence template (min/max/95%-CI accuracy under perturbation). The T&E Engineer shall then upload the bounds file to the MLOps evidence store and request approval from the Dstl security engineer. Approval must be obtained before the Phase 4 gate can open.
U.reasoning: Passive voice obscures actor and timing. In MLOps pipelines, 'filing' should be partially automated (metric export) and partially human (review/approval). Actor (T&E Engineer), trigger (Phase 3 completion), location (evidence store), and approval (Dstl) must be explicit.
U.recommendation: Separate system action (auto-write bounds) from human action (upload, approve). Define: trigger (Phase 3 completion), actor (T&E Engineer), artifact (SACE template), approver (Dstl security engineer), gate (Phase 4 entry).

U.finding_title: Unclear pronoun 'it' in synthetic data provenance: what is 'it'?
U.original_text: Synthetic data provenance chain unverifiable when offline generation omits lineage
U.ambiguity_explanation: 'It' could refer to: data, lineage, generation process, or pipeline. Does 'offline generation' mean: (a) no audit log during execution, (b) generation happens outside versioned repo, (c) generated data lacks metadata? Unclear what 'omits lineage' means: git history? Tool versioning? Data hashes?
U.proposed_rewrite: All synthetic data generated in Phase 3 shall include machine-readable provenance metadata: (1) tool version (TAO, Isaac Sim, StyleGAN), (2) algorithm git commit hash, (3) seed and hyperparameter JSON, (4) input dataset version (base sensor logs or prior synthetic batch), (5) output data file SHA256. This metadata shall be embedded in the SACE evidence artifact and used to reconstruct or verify the generation pipeline on audit. Offline generation (e.g., cloud GPU cluster) shall log all metadata to a central MLOps database and sync to Dstl evidence server within 24 hours.
U.reasoning: Provenance is non-negotiable in safety assurance: auditors must verify data authenticity. Unclear pronoun masks the actual requirement: reproducibility of the data generation pipeline.
U.recommendation: Define provenance as: tool versions, git commit hashes, hyperparameter JSON, base dataset version, output file hash. Store in SACE evidence artifact and MLOps database. Sync to Dstl within 24 hours.

C_complete.finding_title: Missing performance and latency non-functional requirements
C_complete.gap_description: No latency targets for critical operations: (a) requirements decomposition turnaround time unspecified, (b) synthetic data generation time budget missing (impacts Phase 3 schedule), (c) drift detection alert response time (must trigger re-qualification notification within 1 hour per Feature 14, but no system processing latency bound), (d) GSN auto-generation target (requirement says '2 working days', no machine-latency bound).

C_complete.finding_title: Missing security and classified-data handling specifications

C_complete.finding_title: Missing error-condition and invalid-input handling specifications

C_complete.finding_title: Missing detailed ODD dimension definitions and coverage strategy
C_complete.gap_description: Features reference '240 ODD cells' and '≥4 key dimensions' but do NOT specify: (a) exact ODD dimensions (sea state, visibility, vessel class, speed, comms RF quality?), (b) binning strategy (e.g., Beaufort 3-4, visibility 0-50m, 50-200m, >200m), (c) priority weighting (which ODD cells are safety-critical vs nice-to-have?), (d) coverage tolerance (95% coverage target assumes which ODD cells can be unsampled?), (e) ODD boundary validation process (how is Pentland Firth ODD different from Portland Harbour?)

C_complete.finding_title: Missing cost, effort, and resource estimation for PSRQ lifecycle
C_complete.gap_description: Input MD claims '10× more scenarios per engineer-day' and 'GSN drafting reduced from weeks to days' but does NOT specify: (a) total engineer effort for full PSRQ (Phase 1-7), (b) compute resource costs (GPU hours, classified-infrastructure fees), (c) contractor or third-party costs (Isaac Sim licenses, Dstl retainer for 6 reviews), (d) timeline buffer (6 months? 9 months?), (e) contingency for re-work (if Phase 5 uncovers sim-to-real gap, cost to repeat Phase 3+4)

C_complete.finding_title: Missing KPIs and programme health tracking metrics
C_complete.gap_description: Input MD specifies success criteria (ODD coverage ≥95%, robustness bounds filed) but does NOT define: (a) monitoring metrics beyond acceptance criteria, (b) real-time programme health dashboard (e.g., 'ODD coverage % by month'), (c) leading indicators of schedule slip (e.g., synthetic data generation backlog), (d) quality trends (e.g., is adversarial robustness improving or degrading?), (e) cost tracking (are actual GPU costs tracking budget?)

C_complete.finding_title: Missing operator training and human-factors requirements
C_complete.gap_description: Feature 8 mentions 'human-on-the-loop handovers' and 'operator override' with <2-second response time, but does NOT specify: (a) operator skill/training level required, (b) training curriculum (how many hours?), (c) competency validation (what is a passing operator-override test?), (d) situational awareness requirements (must operator understand why auto-stop triggered?), (e) UI/UX design for override interface (single red button? touchscreen? voice?)
C_complete.reasoning: Milarna identified 'Operator training and human factors' as a separate missing finding. Feature 8 assumes 'human intervention <2 seconds' but does not account for training, UI design, or human error rates.

C_complete.finding_title: Missing feedback loop from Article 36 legal review to evidence re-evaluation
C_complete.gap_description: Feature 13 (Article 36 export) assumes legal approval as a gate, but does NOT specify: (a) what happens if legal review rejects GSN (e.g., insufficient operator-override evidence), (b) re-evaluation trigger (does system auto-run Phase 3 with updated requirements?), (c) evidence-gap prioritization (which missing evidence types force Phase 5 re-trial vs just Phase 4 re-simulation?), (d) escalation if gaps unfixable (e.g., UK Defence policy changes during PSRQ), (e) timeline for legal feedback loop (assumed <3 weeks, unspecified)

C_complete.recommendation: Add Legal Feedback Loop Section 3.2.9 with: (i) approval criteria (legal must approve all autonomous-decision logic + override mechanisms), (ii) rejection scenarios (e.g., insufficient evidence of human-override responsiveness), (iii) re-evaluation trigger (if legal rejects, escalate to RAISO + Dstl safety engineer for root-cause analysis), (iv) evidence-gap prioritization (e.g., 'override <2sec unproven' forces Phase 4 re-test, not Phase 3), (v) SLA for legal feedback (submit draft, legal review within 10 working days, re-submit revised within 5 days), (vi) fallback (if legal approval not achievable, RAISO can force deployment with exception rationale).

C_complete.gap_description: Feature 2 (sensor ingestion) ingest 'real sea-trial data' and Feature 10 (physical trials) capture 'real vessel encounters' but do NOT specify: (a) personal-data handling (e.g., fishing-vessel crew names in comms RF logs - GDPR), (b) ship-identification redaction rules (ship names, IMO numbers removable from published datasets?), (c) consent from vessel operators, (d) retention period for identifiable data (is 7-year archive compatible with GDPR right-to-forget?), (e) publication restrictions (which datasets can be released for AI training vs kept classified)

C_complete.finding_title: Missing vendor lock-in risks and AI-tool sustainability plan
C_complete.gap_description: Features depend on 14+ external AI tools (Isaac Sim, NVIDIA TAO, Foolbox, Garak, D-Case Editor, etc.) but do NOT specify: (a) licence sustainability (what if NVIDIA TAO moves from free to paid?), (b) open-source fallbacks (if Isaac Sim discontinued, is Gazebo acceptable?), (c) supply-chain verification (is Garak audited for supply-chain attacks?), (d) tool obsolescence handling (what if OpenSCENARIO 2.0 deprecated?), (e) contingency for tool failure (if D-Case Editor broken mid-GSN generation, can manual authoring complete?)

C_complete.gap_description: Features assume in-service operation (Phase 7, Feature 14) but do NOT specify: (a) what happens when the system is retired (model archive duration), (b) maintenance procedures post-Phase 7 (e.g., if a simulator becomes unsupported), (c) transition plan if PSRQ authority changes (e.g., Dstl Autonomy Lab closes), (d) knowledge transfer (how do we hand over the system to future operators?), (e) cost of long-term archive (7+ year retention per JSP 520 - storage, access, migration)

C_complete.finding_title: Missing NATO/AUKUS interoperability and units/metric harmonisation
C_complete.gap_description: Input MD references 'NATO STANAG 4297/4439/4496' and 'AUKUS Pillar II' but does NOT specify: (a) units for ODD dimensions (Beaufort scale vs wind speed m/s), (b) distance units (nautical miles vs meters), (c) interoperability API (can Australian/Canadian perception stacks consume UK evidence?), (d) data-exchange format standards, (e) classification-marking harmonisation (is UK SECRET equivalent to AUS/CA SECRET?)
